rulings.Case Title: EST. OF ANNE DERRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner as of 9/8/22. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] The administrator's prior attorney was not substituted out until 1998. Is he not seeking any fees for his services prior to the administrator representing herself? He must be given notice or his waiver of fees and notice must be filed. [CRC 7.704(b).] Please submit courtesy copies of the original Petition for Letters of Administration filed on 1/29/21 and the Order Appointing Administrator and Letters of Administration filed on 3/12/91. Because papers prior to 2020 were not scanned and e-filing has resulted in no physical files being available to examiners, papers filed prior to 2020 are not viewable. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE SHIRLEY OUTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The amended petition was file stamped on 7/28/22 but not actually e-filed until 7/29/22, when it was available for viewing. Notice may not be mailed before the [actual] filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (The notice itself was not "amended"; notice should have been just of the amended petition. Also, a Proof of Service -- Civil is never appropriate in Probate. The correct proof of service is the one on the back of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing. If a pleading form of proof of service is used instead of the reverse side of form DE-120, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to the proof of service.) Also, the pleading caption does not disclose the request to waive further notice to the son. [CRC 7.102.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JASON RORK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #9, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) Trustee is requesting termination of the trust and the remaining funds $12,070.33 (as of 4/30/2022) be distributed to John Harper for the benefit of Jason Rork. The same request was made during the 7th account which the court did not approve, and the court allowed the trustee to file an ex parte to put the assets into a blocked account to waive future accounts. Removal of the trust from Court supervision during the lifetime of the conservatee/settlor is generally not favored, whether or not a conservatorship is established or terminated. [LRSF 14.47 B. 6)] 2) $690.00 in bank services charges were incurred $30/$25 per month) for failure to maintain $25,000 in a bank account.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANNE S. OUTIN REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST UNDER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The proof of service filed on 7/28/22 shows that it is an "amended notice" of the Petition Approve Seventh Account, etc., when it should have been a Notice of Hearing of an Amended Petition to Approve Seventh Account, etc. Item 4 of the proof of service is also incomplete (showing the date of mailing of the notice), and the verification shows the year as "26". Also, the pleading caption does not disclose the request to waive further notice to the son. [CRC 7.102.] After these issues are addressed, an appearance likely will be required in connection with the fees not being sought "at this time" -- or ever, since the successor trustee's appointment in 2018 and throughout her subsequent filing of six prior accounting petitions -- such that the trust beneficiaries have no idea if/when fees might be sought and how much they might be. Furthermore, if fees had been sought and paid, the amount of bond might have been reduced. (The fully amended petition appears to have been mostly copied from the initial petition, including all of the errors and omissions in that initial petition, such as the odd figure for the recovery amount of the bond on p. 3, the misspelling of May Findley's last name, as purportedly shown in the will (Findley, not Finley) on p. 4, and the incorrect date for the start of this accounting period on p. 7. There is no need to attach a copy of a prior accounting petition that was filed in this Court. A petition to approve a subsequent accounting should stand on its own and the accounting should simply begin with assets on hand at the end of the prior accounting. In a subsequent accounting, there is no need to refer to all of the I&As.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Counsel has failed to file a response to the Examiner notes since June 21, 2022. a. The status report must be signed and verified by the personal representative. (CRC 7.103) b. Personal representative must give 15 days notice of the petition. c. Petitions need to include sufficient allegations to allow the Court to determine administration is proceeding properly. Please make all necessary allegations as if the petition was for final distribution, including but not limited to: Notice under Probate Code 9202; Creditor's Claims; Assignments/Powers of Attorney; Requests for Special Notice, etc. d. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 11th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JAMES M. ARTHURS TRUST DATED OCTOBER 9, 2015
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Confirmation Of Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The proper venue for a trust petition is the county where the principal place of administration of the trust is located and where the day-to-day activity of the trust is carried on. [Probate Code Sections 17005(a) and 17002(a).] The trustee resides in Medford, Oregon. Did the settlor have a Memoranda of Personal Property? Was James Dale Arthurs not survived by issue? (Exhibit D appears to be a deed, not an account statement.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions To Enforce The No Contest Clause Against Matthew A. Guerra, To Ascertain Beneficiaries Of The Theodore P. Glaza Trust, And
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/31/22. No earlier date available. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] (Not just those entitled to notice.) A proposed order and courtesy copies of any other filings in connection with the petition for instructions or objections thereto must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 10/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HERCULES MOSS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition To Establish First Party Pooled Special Needs Trust, For Order To Execute Joinder Documents, And For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/10/2022 first available date at request of counsel. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit the will. No appearance required. Status on close by September 18, 2023. Frank Mahoney III is appointed probate referee.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN S. JACKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/31/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Item 8 of the petition must identify the trustee of the devisee trust. If the petitioner is the trustee, all trust beneficiaries must be listed in item 8 of the petition and must be clearly identified as such and must receive notice. Item 8 of the petition is missing the alternate executor, who must also be given notice. [Probate Code Sect. 8110(b).] The will was executed by the petitioner as an agent under a durable power of attorney. Please file a copy of the DPA. Courtesy copies of only the petition, the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number, and the proof of publication have been received by the examiner as of 9/7/22. (Item 3g(c) of the petition does not need to be checked when the proposed executor is named as executor in the will.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
